QF.id: XIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_35

question.text: How do dividends affect equity futures pricing versus other asset class futures?

answer.text: Expected dividends reduce equity futures prices below what cost-of-carry alone would predict; currency futures use interest rate differentials (no dividends); commodity futures use storage costs (no dividends)
Dividends affect all derivative markets equally
Dividends have no impact on any futures pricing

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Equity futures: F = S(1+r-d)t where d = dividend yield. Higher dividends mean lower futures price relative to spot. Currency futures: F = S(1+Rd)/(1+Rf) — no dividends, uses two interest rates. Commodity futures: F = S(1+r+s-c)t — uses storage costs and convenience yield, no dividends. **The Trap:** Dividends REDUCE futures prices (carrying the stock earns dividends, reducing net carry cost). This is unique to equity futures. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch3 covers dividend impact on equity futures. This is a key differentiator from currency (I) and IR (IV) futures pricing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Before dividend season, equity futures basis narrows — the expected dividend is already priced in. Unexpected dividend announcements cause immediate futures price adjustments.
